channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Lebanon-Israel Cease-Fire Goes Into Effect
item.description: It was unclear if the agreement between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and the Lebanese government was being honored. Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed militia, acknowledged the temporary truce but did not say if it would abide by it.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Why China Isn’t Pushing Iran to Accept U.S. Demands to End War
item.description: Despite the economic risks from the war, Beijing will likely stick to a hands-off approach. It is wary of being entangled in a conflict it opposed and has little sway over.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo and David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural representing the Strait of Hormuz, on a building in Tehran this week. The strait is a critical passageway for China’s energy and goods.

item.title: Got $100 Million? Ukraine Has Just the Fertilizer Plant for You.
item.description: An effort to privatize the facility is a key test of whether Kyiv can overcome concerns about Russian attacks and corruption to attract foreign investment.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut
item.media:description: The Odesa Portside Plant, one of Europe’s largest fertilizer producers and a crown jewel of Ukrainian industry dating back to Soviet times.

item.title: The Costa Rican Mountain Town Offering Sanctuary to Families Deported by Trump
item.description: In a cloud forest village, a network of residents, foreigners and pacifist Quakers offered a precarious yet vital sanctuary for families expelled by the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and César Rodríguez
item.media:credit: Cesar Rodriguez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Vusala Yusifova and her daughter, Inji, asylum seekers from Azerbaijan, cut a client’s hair in Monteverde, Costa Rica.

item.title: Pope Leo, Amid Standoff With Trump, Says ‘Woe to Those Who Manipulate Religion’
item.description: For days, Pope Leo XIV has attracted criticism from President Trump and his allies for refusing to back the war in Iran. On Thursday, he reiterated his calls for peace.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at Saint Joseph’s Cathedral in Bamenda, Cameroon, on Thursday. A violent secessionist movement centered in the area has divided the country over the past decade.

item.title: Orban’s Defeat Punctures Europe’s Far Right, but Also Offers It a Road Map
item.description: Corruption and economic mismanagement brought down Hungary’s authoritarian leader. Will his political disciples avoid those pitfalls?
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:description: Viktor Orban, center right, in Budapest on Sunday, the night of his election defeat. He was voted out after 16 years in power in Hungary.

item.title: Russian Strikes Kill at Least 15 in Ukraine in Biggest Attack in Months
item.description: Moscow is again ramping up missile and drone attacks on civilian targets, dispelling any notion that a temporary cease-fire for Orthodox Easter might become more lasting.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker and Oleksandra Mykolyshyn
item.media:description: A warehouse hit by a Russian missile strike in Kyiv, Ukraine, on Thursday.

item.title: Pakistan Looks to Play Peacemaker Between U.S. and Iran, Again
item.description: After talks in Islamabad last weekend led to a two-week cease-fire, Pakistan is offering to host another round in an attempt to sustain continuing peace efforts.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, center, and other Pakistani officials during peace talks between the United States and Iran in Islamabad, Pakistan, last week.

item.title: Bahrain Charges Intelligence Officer in the Death of a Detainee
item.description: The officer was charged with assault leading to death, in the case of a man who was accused by Bahraini authorities of espionage during a domestic crackdown tied to the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising over Bahrain’s capital, Manama, after a reported Iranian drone strike last month.

item.title: South African Politician Julius Malema Is Sentenced to 5 Years in Prison
item.description: The leftist lawmaker has been a prime target of President Trump’s attacks on South Africa. The ruling, handed down after a conviction on gun charges, leaves Mr. Malema’s political future in doubt.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon
item.media:description: Julius Malema at a hearing in KuGompo City, South Africa, on Wednesday. Citizens are barred from serving as a lawmaker if they are sentenced to more than 12 months in prison without the option of a fine.

item.title: U.S. Completes Handover of Military Bases in Syria
item.description: The transition signals the end of a formal U.S. military presence in Syria for the first time in more than a decade.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: U.S. forces patrol near the town of Tel Hamees in Syria’s northeastern Hasakah province in 2023. The U.S. military first came to Syria in 2015 to fight the terror group Islamic State.

item.title: Xi Alludes to Trump’s Policies to Make a Case for Closer Ties to Vietnam
item.description: China used a visit by the Vietnamese leader to show a deepening of security ties that analysts say far outpaces U.S. defense ties in the region.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Tung Ngo
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
Ministry of Public Security of the People's Republic of China
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, welcomed President To Lam of Vietnam in Beijing on Wednesday. Mr. Xi said that defending Communist Party rule was a common strategic interest.

item.title: Rohingya Refugee Recounts Rescue From Capsizing Boat That Left Nearly 250 Others Missing
item.description: Hundreds of migrants from Bangladesh, including Rohingya refugees, are feared dead after a boat to Malaysia overturned.
item.dc:creator: Verena Hölzl
item.media:description: A Rohingya survivor being carried on a bamboo stretcher to a hospital at a refugee camp in Cox’s Bazar, Bangladesh, after being rescued last week from a capsized boat.

item.title: Shakespeare Bought One Property in London. Now We Know Exactly Where.
item.description: In confirming the precise location of William Shakespeare’s Blackfriars house, a British scholar raises fresh questions about what he intended to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:credit: Jeremie Souteyrat for The New York Times
item.media:description: William Shakespeare’s property was just across the river from the Globe Theater, left, which staged his plays then and does so today, having been recreated and reopened in 1997.

item.title: Iran War Forces America’s Friends in Asia to Court Its Rivals
item.description: The severe disruption of oil supplies from the Middle East as a result of the war has prompted South Korea and the Philippines to make deals with Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Javier C. Hernández, Choe Sang-Hun and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Ezra Acayan for The New York Times
item.media:description: A gas station in Manila. In the Philippines, America’s oldest treaty ally in Asia, the oil shortage prompted officials to declare a national emergency.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Euan Ward, Johnatan Reiss and Qasim Nauman

item.title: Trump says he might go to Pakistan if an Iran deal were signed there.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporters outside the White House on Thursday.

item.title: 10-Day Cease-Fire Between Israel and Lebanon Goes Into Effect
item.description: The pause in the fighting between Israeli forces and Iranian-allied Hezbollah militants may help smooth the way to a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Isabel Kershner, Eric Schmitt and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday. Hundreds of thousands of Lebanese have been forced by the fighting to leave their homes.

item.title: Bitcoin’s Greatest Mystery
item.description: The creator of Bitcoin has hidden behind the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto for 17 years.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

item.title: The cease-fire goes into effect after Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire.
item.description: Israeli and Lebanese officials had confirmed they would implement the truce. Hezbollah acknowledged the cease-fire, but did not directly address whether it would follow it.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Euan Ward, Tyler Pager and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A man observing the aftermath of an attack in Corniche Mazraa from his home in Beirut on Monday.

item.title: Forced into a corner by the U.S., Netanyahu agrees to a cease-fire in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss

item.title: Mandelson, a Friend of Epstein’s, Became U.S. Ambassador Despite Failing Security Vetting
item.description: Britain’s foreign office overruled vetting officials in granting Peter Mandelson, a friend of Jeffrey Epstein, the highest level of security clearance, the government said.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Peter Mandelson, then Britain’s ambassador to the United States, at the ambassador’s residence in Washington, in February last year.

item.title: Despite Cease-Fire, Iran’s Hackers Haven’t Logged Off
item.description: Tehran’s digital warriors have continued to seek ways to gain an advantage in the conflict in a new phase of cyberspace operations.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Dustin Volz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Tehran this week. Iran is trying to keep up pressure on the United States and Israel with cyberattacks but also positioning itself to mount a bigger retaliation if peace talks do not resume.

item.title: The Long-Term Plan to Scrub Carbon From the Sky
item.description: Microsoft is pulling back from efforts to remove carbon from the atmosphere. But the nascent industry’s proponents say they are thinking in decades, not years.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles
item.media:description: A carbon capture facility in Tracy, Calif., in 2023.

item.title: How Trump’s Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Works
item.description: Our business reporter Peter Eavis breaks down how American military ships have blocked Iranian-linked vessels from using the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. encourages other vessels to make the passage.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Leila Medina, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Stephanie Swart, Sutton Raphael, Josh Holder, Adina Renner and Blacki Migliozzi

item.title: Hegseth Again Threatens Attacks on Iran’s Civilian Infrastructure
item.description: The U.S. blockade of shipments through the Strait of Hormuz would last “for as long as it takes,” the defense secretary said.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We’d rather not have to do it, but we’re ready to go at the command of our president and at the push of a button,” Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Thursday.

item.title: White House Declines to Offer Congress an Estimate of Iran War Cost
item.description: Russell T. Vought, the White House budget director, said the fluctuating nature of the U.S.-Israeli war made it difficult to pinpoint expenses.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Russell T. Vought, the White House budget director, said he would not give an estimate of the cost of the conflict because he did not want to be inaccurate.

item.title: A Timeline of the Lebanon-Israel Relationship
item.description: Israeli and Lebanese officials this week met in Washington for direct talks between two nations, which have technically been at war since 1948.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Workers removing scrap metal in Beirut last week from the rubble left by Israeli bombardment.

item.title: White House Shrugs Off Shaky Economy as War Exceeds Trump’s Timeline
item.description: Stocks may be soaring again, but the war in Iran has started to pinch the finances of many Americans.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
White House Council of Economic Advisers
item.media:description: The energy surge has made groceries more expensive while also raising costs for farmers.

item.title: Sri Lanka Uses U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire to Repatriate Iranian Sailors
item.description: Sri Lanka had been refusing to send back the Iranian Navy personnel, including survivors from a warship that the United States torpedoed, during active hostilities.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das and Pamodi Waravita
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A photograph provided by the Sri Lankan Navy showed one of its vessels approaching an Iranian Navy vessel, the Bushehr, during a rescue operation off the coast of Colombo in March.

item.title: What the U.S. Blockade Means for Iran’s Economy
item.description: Putting a stop to Iranian shipping will further add to Iran’s economic pain, analysts said, but it might not be enough to force concessions or lessen the global energy crunch.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Despite sanctions, Iran conducted about $110 billion in non-oil trade with over 170 nations last year, importing goods including smartphones, auto parts and grain.

item.title: Marco Rubio and Lebanese President Discuss Cease-Fire Efforts
item.description: President Trump had said that Israeli and Lebanese leaders would speak directly, but a Lebanese official said the country’s president had rejected the idea.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Francesca Regalado
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Destruction at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: Carbon Removal Industry Reels as Microsoft Retreats
item.description: Once held up as a key solution to climate change, a field that aims to remove carbon from the atmosphere is struggling to catch on.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles
item.media:credit: Ariana Gomez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Workers at a direct air capture facility under construction in Texas in 2024. Hundreds of start-ups developing carbon removal technologies have raised more than $5 billion in recent years.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Tyler Pager, Johnatan Reiss and Pranav Baskar

item.title: 3 Killed in Boat Strike in the Pacific, Pentagon Says
item.description: It was the third such attack by the U.S. military in three days, and the 51st attack in a campaign against people who the United States has accused of smuggling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Francis L. Donovan at a confirmation hearing in January to become the leader of U.S. Southern Command.

item.title: China’s G.D.P. Stronger Than Expected, Led by Infrastructure Spending
item.description: A steep slide in housing prices has left consumers less prosperous and less willing to spend, but the government is pouring money into new rail lines and other projects.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: An import and export trade show in southern China this week.

item.title: Iran Will Be in U.S. for World Cup, FIFA’s Infantino Says
item.description: The comments come as peace talks between Iran and the United States have not yet resumed and a cease-fire is set to expire next week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.media:description: Iran tried unsuccessfully to move its matches from the United States to Mexico.

item.title: Sudan Enters Fourth Year of War Amid World’s Most Severe Humanitarian Crisis
item.description: Fighting that erupted in 2023 has forced millions from their homes and left widespread hunger that is now being compounded by strife in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Declan Walsh
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: A 2-month-old patient, Saidal Altaher, getting treatment for malnutrition on Wednesday in Port Sudan, Sudan.

item.title: Trump’s Portrayal of the War in Iran Collides With Reality
item.description: President Trump is confronting a crisis that is not bending to his narrative of a “pretty reasonable” new regime in Iran and all-but-assured victory for the United States.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in Miami last week. Mr. Trump has declared a “regime change” accomplishment in Iran, even though analysts believe the war may have only increased the internal sway of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.

item.title: U.K.’s Starmer Summons U.S. Social Media Companies Over Child Safety Online
item.description: The British prime minister will meet with executives from Meta, Google and other companies on Thursday as his government explores how to protect children from online harms.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain speaking about online safety at a school in London last month.

item.title: Iran could struggle to enforce all of its latest shipping threats, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. Traffic is still at a fraction of its usual levels.

item.title: The Pope in Africa
item.description: The continent is crucial to the future of the church, but it’s also the center of long-running tensions over some of Catholicism’s most charged issues.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.media:credit: Daniel Beloumou Olomo/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Cameroon’s capital, Yaoundé, yesterday.

item.title: Pakistani Mediators Arrive in Tehran in Bid to Keep U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Alive
item.description: The diplomacy came as the U.S. Navy locked down trade to Iranian ports, and Iran responded by threatening critical shipping routes across the region.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Eric Schmitt, Jenny Gross and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel at the Strait of Hormuz, off the coast of Oman’s Musandam province, on Sunday.

item.title: Saudi Fund to Back Away From LIV Golf Under Mounting Financial Pressures
item.description: The Saudi league, established in 2022, attracted some of the sport’s biggest stars with huge contracts.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch, Vivian Nereim and Alan Blinder
item.media:description: Yasir al-Rumayyan, left, the head of Saudi Arabia’s sovereign wealth fund, presenting a trophy at the LIV Golf championship in Michigan in 2025.

item.title: Getting the Pope to Answer a Pointed Question at 30,000 Feet
item.description: The Times’s Rome bureau chief is still getting a feel for a pontiff who is willing to challenge President Trump and use his sense of humor.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV, flanked by press minders, spoke to journalists during a flight to Algeria on Monday.

item.title: South Africa Names Roelf Meyer, Apartheid-Era Negotiator, as U.S. Ambassador
item.description: The move comes as the country looks to strengthen diplomatic ties with the United States after months of hostility between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane
item.media:description: Roelf Meyer, center, with President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa to his right at an event in Pretoria in August. Mr. Meyer was appointed ambassador to the United States on Wednesday.

item.title: With Iran War on Pause, Political Battle Resumes Over Israeli Democracy
item.description: A court fight over Itamar Ben-Gvir, an extremist who oversees the police, pits the attorney general against Benjamin Netanyahu and his right-wing government.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: Israeli Supreme Court justices on Wednesday before hearing a petition to force Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to oust the national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir.

item.title: Top U.S. Diplomat in Venezuela Leaves Post After Arriving in January
item.description: Laura Dogu, who reopened the U.S. Embassy in Venezuela, returns to advising Gen. Dan Caine, the highest ranking U.S. military officer, as the war with Iran continues.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero
item.media:description: Laura Dogu, the top U.S. diplomat in Venezuela, last month in the capital, Caracas.

item.title: After Fighting for the Rights of Workers, Brazil’s President Lula Faces a Labor Abuse Crisis
item.description: The dismissal of Brazil’s top labor inspector has landed the leftist government of President Lula, a former union leader, in an awkward spot.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Victor Moriyama for The New York Times
item.media:description: A factory for the automaker BYD under construction last year in Camaçari, Bahia, Brazil. A labor ministry inspection found that Chinese workers building the plant were living in squalid conditions.

item.title: ‘We Cannot Lose Sight of Ukraine.’ Europeans Promise More Aid to Kyiv.
item.description: The focus in recent weeks has been on the conflict in Iran, but European leaders said it was crucial to keep aid flowing to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary general, met with the defense ministers of Ukraine, Germany and Britain in Berlin on Wednesday.

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum’s War on Crime in Mexico Faces a Grim Reckoning: 133,000 Missing People
item.description: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico is under pressure to end one of her country’s most painful tragedies: the disappearance of more than 133,000 people.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Fred Ramos for The New York Times
item.media:description: Izaguirre ranch in Teuchitlán, Jalisco, where volunteer groups found charred human remains. The Jalisco New Generation Cartel had been using it as a training camp for its new recruits.

item.title: BBC Announces Plan to Cut Around 2,000 Jobs
item.description: The cuts amount to about 10 percent of the work force at the British public broadcaster, which is facing financial pressures.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: The headquarters of the British Broadcasting Corporation in London.

item.title: Debt Alarms Ring as Countries Rack Up More Emergency Spending
item.description: Amid signs of a prolonged period of high energy costs, policymakers are urging restraint as governments open up the public till to protect households and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, second from left, with other officials at a news briefing in Berlin this week.

item.title: Israel Considering Cease-Fire With Lebanon, Officials Say
item.description: Talk of a truce that would pause the war between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon followed rare direct talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ronen Bergman, Aaron Boxerman and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Residents collecting scrap metal from a power station destroyed by Israeli airstrikes in Tyre, Lebanon, earlier this month. While Israel recently scaled back attacks on Beirut, the Lebanese capital, it has continued striking the southern part of the country.

item.title: Kuwait Detained Journalist After Comments on Iran War Images, Watchdog Says
item.description: The country has not commented publicly about Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, who the Committee to Protect Journalists said had been charged with spreading false information.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ahmed Shihab‑Eldin in Qatar in November. He has not posted online or been seen in public since early March.

item.title: Iran-Linked Ships Slow or Stop as U.S. Navy Enforces Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. Navy warships are enforcing a blockade on all vessels from all nations entering or leaving coastal areas or ports in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. Traffic is still at a fraction of its usual levels.

item.title: Trump Breaks With Meloni, Italy’s Leader, Amid Dispute Over Pope and Iran
item.description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was once considered one of President Trump’s closest European allies. Their friendship now appears in danger.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump greeting Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy at the White House last year. They were once considered close allies.
